docs.title: Fortitude Barbell
docs.text: Learn how to set up Youtube and OBS for 4K streaming, including API setup, stream key management, and optimal settings for high-quality broadcasts.

docs.title: Software Required
docs.text:
These are all free, and are available either on the links, or in the shared OneDrive folder Fortitude Barbell\Streaming-Release\Software

  • OBS – Open Broadcaster Software for live streaming and recording
    • OBS Filter - obs-shaderfilter - Open Broadcaster Software Plugin for filter borders
  • Davinci Resolve – Professional video editing
  • Iriun WebCam – Use upto 4 Android/iPhones as separate distinct WiFi webcam devices for your computer

These are the mobile apps for Iriun for iPhone or Android

  • Iriun Webcam for Android (Google Play) – Android app for Iriun Webcam
  • Iriun Webcam for iOS (App Store) – iOS app for Iriun Webcam

docs.title: Software Recommendation
docs.text:
These are recommended, but have a one time cost, albiet not too expensive, it's frequently on sale every 6 months or so, and doesnt require an ongoing subscription

  • Affinity V2 Universal License – Professional photo, design, and publishing software

docs.title: Profile Settings
docs.text:
All the settings below will be stored as a Profile.

  • Always create a new profile, so that existing stream profile settings are not lost

  • Profiles do not store the Scenes or Sources; these are stored under Scene Collection

OBS Profiles can be Exported and Imported

docs.title: Stream Settings
docs.text:
• OBS Settings MUST use a stream key for 4K
  • Connect Account option will NOT use a custom 4K Stream Key

docs.title: Output Settings
docs.text:
Output settings will greatly depend on hardware CPU + GPU, these are the minimum settings

  • Output Mode Advanced
  • Audio Track - 1
  • H.264 or H.265 (Hardware Required, maybe AMD or Nvidia, or even Intel)
  • Bitrate -- 20000 Kbps
  • Preset -- Balanced

4K is not possible on old hardware.

docs.title: Audio Settings
docs.text:
Default must be changed to a lower value for Track 1 to match YouTube recommendations

  • Audio Bitrate - 128

docs.title: OBS Canvas
docs.text:
Set both - Base Canvas (Preview) and - Output (Streaming)

to 3840x2160 (4K/2160p) resolution.

It can be manually typed in if the dropdown box does not show it (Which will be due to physical monitor resolution limitations)

FPS will be determined by Hardware, 60 is only possible if the hardware is capable

docs.title: What is an OBS Scene Collection?
docs.text:
Scene Collections handle the various overlays, artifacts, and logos that appear on screen. They can be:

  • Imported/exported between computers
  • Created from scratch

This guide covers both methods.

docs.title: What are Scenes?
docs.text:
In OBS, a "scene" is a collection of sources such as

  • video feeds
  • images
  • overlays
  • text

These are arranged and layered to create a specific layout for your broadcast.

Each scene can be switched live during a stream, allowing you to transition between different camera angles, graphics, or content setups seamlessly.

Scenes are essential for organizing your stream's visual elements and making your production look professional.

Below is an typical completed scene setup, showing all the scenes used for a typical competition stream:

docs.title: What are Sources?
docs.text:
In OBS, a "source" is any input that you add to a scene such as

  • a video capture device
  • images
  • text
  • browser window
  • audio input

Sources are the building blocks of each scene, and you can layer, resize, crop, and filter them to create your desired layout. Each scene can have multiple sources, and sources can be reused across different scenes for efficiency and consistency.

Below is an example showing the typical sources used in the main competition platform scene:

docs.title: What are Groups?
docs.text:
Below is an example of how the groupings of source elements are linked to elements on the broadcast

Elements, or Groups of Elements can be hidden or un-hidden to create different layouts within the same Scene in this example

docs.title: Import an Existing Scene Collection
docs.text:
• File → Scene Collection → Import

  • Select 'Add'

  • Locate the shared OneDrive Location

docs.title: Create a New Scene Collection
docs.text:
• File → Scene Collection → New
  • Enter a descriptive name (e.g., "FB_Competition_4K")

docs.title: Sources
docs.text:
Purpose:

Pre-competition video reel showing sponsors

Copyright-free background music

Setup:

Create new scene: Starting Soon

Add sources:

    Browser Source: YouTube countdown
    URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hbK5C_DxiKk

    Media Source: Sponsor reel video
    Path: OneDrive/FortitudeBarbell/Streaming-Release/2160p-4K/Videos/Sponsor-Video-Reel.mov
    (Enable loop option)

Audio Check:

Ensure output is not muted
Adjust volume via OBS mixer

docs.title: Main Platform Scene
docs.text:
Purpose:

Primary competition view with:

    Platform camera
    Competitor stats
    Sponsor logos

docs.title: Source Filters
docs.text:
Setup:

Background

    Add Color Source (3840×2160, Dark Blue #ff00006c)

Organize in Groups (bottom-to-top order):
text

- Test Patterns
- VideoSources
- Sponsors
- LiftingCast


Key Elements:

Component Details Image Reference Test Patterns Test-Pattern-4K.png + Test-Image-4K.png Platform Camera USB/Hollyland device (3840×2160) LiftingCast Stats https://liftingcast.com/meets//liveFeed/version3 LiftingCast Lights https://liftingcast.com/meets//liveFeed/version1 

Filters:

    Color Key: Remove black backgrounds (#000000)

    Crop/Pad: Isolate key elements

        Stats: Left 1240px, Right 1500px

        Lights: Left 242px, Right 3421px


Transitions:

    Apply swipe animations (Left for Show, Right for Hide)

docs.title: What is 4K?
docs.text:
4K (2160p) refers to an ultra-high-definition video resolution of 3840×2160 pixels, delivering:

  • 4× the detail of 1080p HD
  • Sharper image quality
  • More immersive viewing experience (especially on large screens)

docs.title: YouTube Requirements
docs.text:
• ✅ Special 4K-enabled streaming key
  • ✅ Support for high frame rates (up to 60fps)
  • 🚫 Standard keys limit streams to lower resolutions (1080p or lower)

docs.title: Hardware Considerations
docs.text:
• High upload bandwidth (≥30 Mbps recommended)
  • Powerful CPU/GPU for encoding (Typically NVidia or AMD)
  • 4K-capable Video Sources (ie Platform Camera, HDMI TX/RX devices)
  • 4K-capable capture devices (The HDMI input device attached via USB-C)

docs.title: Internet Speed
docs.text:
Ensure there is enough upload bandwidth to use 4K streaming. Minimum Upload Speed for 4K Streaming is >=30 Mbps

https://www.speedtest.net/

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text:
• Logged in as Fortitude Barbell
  • Preferred: A channel manager with your own Gmail account
  • YouTube Channel Settings to add a Manager Role

Youtube Studio

docs.title: Existing 4K API Key
docs.text:
YouTube requires a 4K enabled Streaming Key There should be a key already available that allows 4K (2160p) resolution and up to 60 Frames per second

  • Click Create → Go Live Youtube Studio

  • Copy the 4K Stream Key for OBS

docs.title: New 4K API Key
docs.text:
YouTube requires a 4K enabled Streaming Key ONLY If the key is NOT available perform this step

  • Click Create → Go Live

  • Create a new key with manual settings and enable 2160p
